Items.Title: 宣布 DEV 上的 Redis 黑客松！ | Announcing the Redis Hackathon on DEV!
Items.Description:
你好，亲爱的开发社区！

我们想停下来，让您了解一些令人兴奋的消息：今天，我们推出了一种新方式，让您可以在开放的环境中构建一个有趣的应用程序，学习新知识，并在此过程中赢得激动人心的奖品……

在 DEV 上宣布 Redis Hackathon 🎉

如果您熟悉我们在 DEV 上的黑客马拉松，您就会知道社区对他们有很多乐趣，并且他们构建的内容非常有创意。 无论您过去是否加入过我们，我们都希望您能通过参加 DEV 上的 Redis 黑客松来投入您的心血！

继续阅读以获取有关如何参加本次比赛的所有详细信息、有关 Redis 的更多信息以及他们为我们的社区提供的奖品。

为什么参加？ 在过去的几年里，Redis 已经从一个缓存数据库发展成为一个多模型主数据库。 这意味着您的缓存现在就是您的数据库，而您的数据库就是您的缓存！

其次，因为它是多模型的，所以您可以使用它来收集数十亿个事件（如 Kafka）、存储 JSON 文档（如 MongoDB）、查询和索引数据（如 Algolia 或 Elasticsearch）、分析时间序列数据（如 InfluxDB）和推荐（如 Neo4j）——您可以在一个系统中完成所有操作并快速完成。

此外，如果您使用的是微服务架构，则可以使用 Redis 连接所有这些服务，并以并行或有序的方式快速处理请求和响应。

对于本次黑客马拉松，您将构建一个使用其中许多功能的新应用程序，或者迁移现有的 OSS 演示应用程序并展示降低的复杂性和改进的性能。

看看这个 6 分钟的视频：

从现在到 2022 年 8 月 29 日，DEV 与 Redis 合作举办社区黑客马拉松，让您有机会使用 Redis 构建新应用程序或简化复杂的后端。 任何提交有效项目（包括在 DEV 上发布的官方提交帖子）的人都将自动进入，以赢得各种精彩奖品（包括高达 2,000 美元）。

项目类别

无需创建新应用，只需移植现有应用的后端
笔记：
使用 Redis 的搜索和 JSON 模块进行 CRUD 操作。 使用Redis Gears将 Redis 用作前端数据库并将更新异步发送到 MongoDB（ Mongo 示例）。 在此处了解有关使用 Redis 和 Mongo 的不同方式（缓存、后写或直写）的更多信息。 最后，在您的提交中展示使用和不使用 Redis 的架构图以及性能改进。
古怪的通配符：构建一个不属于上述类别之一的随机应用程序。 对于这个类别，我们正在寻找一些真正愚蠢和/或有趣的提交。 自由地梦想远大而荒谬的梦想——并利用 Redis 提供的任何产品。

提示：如果您仍在努力想出一些东西，请关注Redis Org on DEV ，并留意他们的团队将分享的即将到来的项目想法和其他资源！

开发者贴纸包
  • 亚军奖品（共10个）：

    • 开发者贴纸包
    • 参与者（有一个有效的项目）：

      • 开发者贴纸包
      • 奖金 #1 ：我们将为所有在提交过程中提供 5 分钟视频的大奖获得者和亚军（最多 14 名获奖者）额外奖励 200 美元。

        奖励#2 ：Redis 将在社交媒体上宣传获奖者和顶级提交者的应用程序，并邀请他们在 Redis 官方网站上撰写客座博客，以宣传您和您的成就。 这些应用程序也将出现在Redis Launchpad上

        如何参加 DEV 上的 Redis Hackathon

        一定要使用云中的Redis Stack数据库。

        • 创建一个免费的 Redis 数据库，或使用特殊代码DEVTO500在具有更大存储空间的付费帐户上获得 500 美元的信用额度（如果您的项目具有大型数据集，您将希望采用这条路线）。 请注意，您需要选择付费计划才能使用此优惠券
        • 如果您不想使用云，则可以安装 Redis Stack Docker 映像。

        使用以下语言之一：

        • 重要提示：使用此帖子模板正式提交您的黑客马拉松申请。 请务必解决模板中的每个提示和说明。

          奖励积分！ 作为您项目的可选补充，我们邀请您创建提交帖子的视频版本！

          我们的评委将向任何创建 5-10 分钟可公开观看的 YouTube 视频解释其项目的人授予“奖励积分”*，该视频可以嵌入到您在 DEV 上的提交帖子中（模板中的说明）。

          在这个视频中，你必须...

          • 谈论您的应用程序/项目
          • 告诉我们您的项目如何存储和读取数据以及数据建模是什么样的
          • 解释你的应用程序的架构
          • 使用RedisInsight显示一些数据，
          • 并在说明第一行的视频说明中添加指向https://redis.info/try-free-dev-to的链接
          • 项目考虑中的“奖励积分”意味着我们的评委在选择获胜者时将首先考虑具有可选视频插件的其他有效项目。

          其他资源

          如果您仍有疑问，请随时在帮助线程或 Redis Discord中提问。

          附加说明和规则：

          • 我们鼓励您使用#redishackathon标签在 DEV 上分享更新帖子，以让我们了解您的进度（提示：在所有与 Redis Hackathon 相关的帖子的降价标题中使用series: [“series name”]链接所有内容一系列的）
          • 允许多次提交
          • 如果您与任何人合作，请在您的提交帖子中列出他们的 DEV 句柄，以便我们向您的整个团队颁发个人资料徽章！ 谢谢你的理解！

          无需购买。 仅对 18 岁以上开放。 比赛报名截止日期为 2022 年 8 月 29 日晚上 11:59（世界标准时间）。 在法律或法规禁止或限制的情况下，比赛无效。 所有参赛作品必须是新项目并在黑客马拉松期间创建。 有关官方规则，请参阅比赛公告页面和一般比赛官方规则。

          社区支持

          要询问有关 Redis 或本次比赛规则的任何问题，请在Redis Hackathon 帮助线程中发表评论。 我们的团队将与 Redis 团队合作监控此空间以回答您的问题。

          需要一些外部动力和指导吗？ 谁没有！ 我们鼓励所有参与者在我们的社区讨论线程中摇摆不定，您可以在其中分享您的想法并在构建应用程序时从 DEV 社区获得有关改进的建议。 您还可以使用此线程分享您在获得他人支持的过程中取得的进展。

          重要日期🗓

          • 获胜者将在最终提交截止日期后的几周内被选中并宣布。

            我们很高兴您能与我们在 Redis 的朋友一起参加这个全新的黑客马拉松。 玩得开心，学到很多东西，并在此过程中随时通知我们。

            祝你好运，编码愉快！ 🍀 🍀



    • 原文:

    Hello, dear DEV community!

    We wanted to stop by and let you in on some exciting news: today, we’re launching a new way for you to build an interesting application in the open, learn something new, and win exciting prizes in the process…

    Announcing the Redis Hackathon on DEV 🎉

    If you’re familiar with our hackathons here on DEV, you know that the community has a lot of fun with them and gets pretty creative with what they build. Whether you’ve joined us in the past or not, we hope you’ll throw your hat into the ring by participating in the Redis Hackathon on DEV!

    Keep reading to get all the details on how you can enter this contest, more about Redis, and the prizes they’re offering our community.

    Why Participate? What's so interesting about Redis beyond caching?

    Redis is the most loved open source database in the world! Over the last several years Redis has evolved from a caching database into a multi-model primary database. This means your cache is now your database, and your database is your cache!

    Secondly, because it’s multi-model, you can use it for collecting billions of events (like Kafka), storing JSON documents (like MongoDB), querying and indexing your data (like Algolia or Elasticsearch), analyzing time series data (like InfluxDB), and recommending (like Neo4j) – you can do it all in a single system and do it fast.

    Redis gives you unprecedented flexibility to build anything you want, or just simplify a complex backend that has multiple data systems(Kafka + MongoDB + Algolia + InfluxDB + Neo4j + AnalyticsDB) with just a single system (Redis).

    Furthermore, if you are using a microservices architecture, you can use Redis to connect all those services and process requests and responses blazing fast in both parallel or in-order fashion.

    For this hackathon, you’ll be building a new app that uses many of these capabilities or migrate an existing OSS demo app and show reduced complexity and improved performance.

    P.S. Want to get a sense of the benefits of Redis before you get started? Take a look at this 6 minute video:

    From now through August 29th, 2022, DEV has partnered up with Redis for a community hackathon that will give you the chance to build a new application using Redis or simplify a complex backend. Anyone who submits a valid project (including an official submission post, published on DEV) will be automatically entered to win a variety of fantastic prizes (including up to $2,000 USD).

    Project Categories

    The Redis Hackathon on DEV is calling for projects in the following four categories:

    • Minimalism Magicians: Simplify an existing OSS reference demo app from one of the cloud providers (AWS, Azure, GCP etc) that uses multiple data systems (such as ElastiCache, TimeStream, DynamoDB, Neptune, Kafka, etc) by replacing as many of the data systems as possible with Redis.

    Notes for the “Minimalism Magicians” category:
    • AWS examples: Samples repo, IoT Reference Architectures, Retail Demo Store
    • Google Cloud examples: Samples repo , GCP Microserves demo
    • Azure examples: Samples repo, Streaming at scale, Search with JavaScript. No need to create a new app, just port the backend of an existing app
    • Be sure to show a before and after architecture diagram with a working demo application.
    • Be sure to show the performance improvement between the new architecture and the old architecture.

    • Microservice Mavens: Build an event-driven microservices application that primarily uses Redis features such as streams, pubsub, queues, JSON, search, time-series, etc. to provide a blazing fast yet simple platform. (Inspiration: GCP Microserves demo

    Notes for the “Microservice Mavens” category:
    • Video explaining how to use Redis with Microservices
    • Recently published e-book: Understanding Streams in Redis and Kafka – A Visual Guide

    • MEAN/MERN Mavericks: Build a new (or port an existing OSS) MEAN/MERN stack app and use Redis in one of the following three ways.

    • Use Redis to your MEAN/MERN stack to add caching and advanced searching capabilities using JSON and Search modules.
    • Use Redis as a primary database instead of MongoDB (i.e. replace “M” in MEAN/MERN with “R” for Redis).
    • Use Redis along side MongoDB as a frontend database (“Write-behind” or “Write-Through”) in front of MongoDB (i.e. do all the CRUD operations directly with Redis and asynchronously send the updates back to Mongo for storage).

    Notes:
    Use Redis' Search and JSON modules for CRUD operations. Use Redis Gears to use Redis as a frontend database and asynchronously send updates to MongoDB ( Mongo Example ). Learn more about different ways (Cache-aside, Write-behind or Write-Through) of using Redis and Mongo here. Lastly, show the architecture diagram with and without Redis and performance improvements in your submission.
    (Inspiration: Redis Launchpad.

    • Wacky Wildcards: Build a random app that doesn’t fit into one of the categories above. With this category, we are looking for some truly silly and/or fun submissions. Feel free to dream big and ridiculously — and utilize any offering that Redis offers.

    Tip: If you are still struggling to come up with something, please follow the Redis Org on DEV and look out for upcoming project ideas and additional resources that their team will be sharing!

    💰 Prizes 💰

    4 Grand Prize Winners (one in each category):

    • $2,000 USD gift card or equivalent
    • $300 USD credit to the Forem Shop
    • DEV Sticker Pack
    • DEV “Redis Hackathon” Grand Prize profile badge

    Runner-Up Prizes (10 Total):

    • $200 USD gift card or equivalent
    • $150 USD credit to the Forem Shop
    • DEV Sticker Pack
    • DEV “Redis Hackathon” Runner-Up profile badge

    Participants (with a valid project):

    • DEV Sticker Pack
    • DEV “Redis Hackathon” participant profile badge

    Bonus #1: We will be awarding $200 USD extra for all Grand Prize winners as well as Runners-Up that have a 5 minutes video as part of the submission (max 14 winners).

    Bonus #2: Redis will promote the winners and top submitters’ apps over social media, and invite them to write guest blogs on the Redis official website to promote you and your accomplishments. The apps will also be featured on the Redis Launchpad

    How to Participate in the Redis Hackathon on DEV

    1) Sign up for a free Redis Cloud account by clicking here. Be sure to use the Redis Stack database in the cloud.

    • Create a free Redis database, or use the special code DEVTO500 to get a $500 credit on a paid account with a larger storage (-- you’ll want to go this route if your project features a large dataset). Please note that you’ll need to choose a paid plan to use this coupon
    • If you prefer not to use the cloud, you can install the Redis Stack Docker image instead.

    2) Create a new and original app during the contest period for mobile, desktop, web, or CLI using Redis that falls under one of the categories listed above. Use one of the following languages:

    • C#/ASP.NET Core
    • Java/Spring
    • JS/TS/Node.js
    • Python

    3) Use one of the following permissive licenses for your code: MIT, Apache, BSD-2, BSD-3, or Commons Clause.

    4) Share your code publicly on GitHub and ensure that your repository:

    5) IMPORTANT: Use this post template to officially submit your application for the hackathon. Be sure to address every prompt and instruction in the template.

    7) Be sure to publish your submission on DEV between August 1st and August 29th, 2022 (@ 11:59 PM UTC), and provide your app’s URL, screenshot, description, and source code

    ⚠️ Heads-up that you'll only be able to view our submission template linked above if you're logged into DEV.

    Bonus Points! (additional $200 prize per winner)

    As an optional supplement to your project, we invite you to create a video version of your submission post!

    Our judges will be awarding “bonus points”* to anyone who creates a 5-10 minute publicly viewable YouTube video explaining their project, which can be embedded into your submission post on DEV (instructions in the template).

    In this video you must…

    • Talk about your app/project
    • Tell us how your project stores and reads the data and what the data modeling is like
    • Explain the architecture of your app
    • Show some of your data using RedisInsight,
    • And add a link to https://redis.info/try-free-dev-to in the video description in the first line of the description
    • “Bonus points” in project consideration means that our judges will consider otherwise valid projects with the optional video add-on FIRST when selecting winners.

    Additional Resources

    The Redis team has put together some resources to help you quickly get started. If you still have questions, feel free to ask them in the help thread or Redis Discord.

    Additional Notes and Rules:

    • We encourage you to share update posts on DEV using the #redishackathon tag to keep us posted on your progress (hint: use series: [“series name”] in the markdown heading of all your Redis Hackathon-related posts to link all content in a series)
    • Multiple submissions are allowed
    • If you collaborate with anyone, please list their DEV handles in your submission post so we can award a profile badge to your entire team! DEV does not handle prize-splitting, so in the event your project is named a Grand Prize-winner or runner-up, you will need to split those amongst yourselves. Thank you for understanding!

    NO PURCHASE NECESSARY. Open only to 18+. Contest entry period ends August 29th, 2022, 11:59 PM UTC. Contest is void where prohibited or restricted by law or regulation. All entries must be new projects and created during the hackathon period. For Official Rules, see Contest Announcement Page and General Contest Official Rules.

    Community Support

    To ask any questions about Redis or about the rules of this contest, leave a comment in the Redis Hackathon help thread. Our team will be monitoring this space to answer your questions in collaboration with the Redis team.

    Need some external motivation and guidance? Who doesn’t! We encourage all participants to swing by our community discussion thread where you can share your ideas and get suggestions on improvements from the DEV community as you build your app. You can also use this thread to share your progress along the way to get support from others.

    Important Dates 🗓

    • August 1, 2022: Hackathon Begins
    • August 29, 2022: Hackathon Submission Due at 11:59 PM UTC
    • August 30, 2022: Submission judging begins

    Winners will be selected and announced in the weeks following the final submission deadline.

    We’re so excited for you to join us for this brand new hackathon with our friends at Redis. Have fun, learn lots, and keep us posted along the way.

    Good luck and happy coding! 🍀

Items.Title: 🍔 单滑块：通过克隆对多个帐户直接收费 | 🍔 Single Slider: Direct Charges on Multiple Accounts with Cloning
Items.Description:
成绩单

欢迎来到另一个 TSE 单滑块！

这都是关于克隆多个帐户的直接费用。 我将解释如何从客户那里收集一次付款详细信息，并将其用于多个关联账户的多次直接费用。

在平台上设置

让我们从创建或使用现有Customer的平台开始。 我们将创建一个与此客户关联的SetupIntent ，当我们确认 SetupIntent 客户端时，我们会得到一个已设置以供将来使用的PaymentMethod 。 确认成功后，SetupIntents 会自动将关联的 PaymentMethod 附加到关联的 Customer，从而允许重复使用 PaymentMethod。

在我们继续之前的一个快速说明：对于这张幻灯片，我使用带有序列号的示例对象 ID（例如pm_1 、 pm_2等）。 通常我使用随机示例 ID 来近似 Stripe 将生成的随机 ID，但在这张幻灯片中使用顺序 ID 会让事情更容易理解。 请记住，当您实际使用 Stripe 时，为这些对象生成的 ID 将是随机的，它们都不会是连续的或以任何方式相关的。

发送克隆

好的，现在我们已经设置了 PaymentMethod 以供将来在平台上使用，我们可以将其克隆到连接的帐户。 让我们从关联帐户acct_a开始，我们要在其中进行第一次直接收费。

要将 PaymentMethod 克隆到这个连接的帐户，我们需要向/v1/payment_methods发出POST请求。 我们将Stripe-Account标头的值设置为连接帐户的 ID，在请求的正文中，我们将指定要克隆的 PaymentMethod 的 ID，因为该 PaymentMethod 附加到客户，我们还需要指定客户 ID。

当此 API 请求成功时，将在已连接帐户上创建一个新的 PaymentMethod 对象。 与平台上的pm_1一样，这个新的 PaymentMethod ( pm_2 ) 表示相同的支付信息，但除此之外，它是一个全新的独立对象。

我们现在可以使用这个克隆的 PaymentMethod 通过创建并确认 PaymentIntent 来在连接的帐户上创建直接费用！

确保正确设置

请注意，已连接帐户上的克隆 PaymentMethod 继承了由平台上的 SetupIntent 执行的设置以供将来使用。 这意味着无需在已连接的帐户上再次进行设置。

这里要记住的一件事是平台所在的国家/地区和关联帐户。 您可能需要在on_behalf_of上使用 on_behalf_of 以正确设置 PaymentMethod 以用于您连接的帐户。 例如，如果您的平台帐户在美国，3D Secure 很少发生或从不发生，SetupIntent 执行的设置可能不会提示进行 3D Secure 身份验证，因此如果您想直接收费就不够了执行SCA的欧洲关联账户。

如果您要在与您的平台帐户不同的国家/地区创建直接费用，您可以在on_behalf_of上使用 on_behalf_of 来指定将在同一国家/地区进行收费的连接帐户，以确保执行正确的设置。

第二个连接账户，更直接的费用

现在让我们看一下第二个连接的帐户acct_b 。 如果我们还想使用来自平台的相同 PaymentMethod 来创建另一个帐户的直接费用怎么办？ 它的工作方式基本相同，从克隆 PaymentMethod 的相同 API 请求开始，唯一的变化是我们在Stripe-Account标头中指定的帐户 ID。

一旦这个 API 请求成功，我们就会在acct_b上有另一个新的克隆 PaymentMethod ( pm_3 )。 这个 PaymentMethod 也继承了平台的设置，所以我们可以用同样的方式创建一个直接收费。

现在让我们想象一下，我们想在acct_b上创建第二个直接费用。 您可能认为我们可以为此重用pm_3 ，但有两个原因不起作用。

首先，我们不会将已连接帐户上的克隆 PaymentMethods 附加到客户（这可以重复使用），这意味着它们只能使用一次，并且我们已经使用pm_3进行直接收费。

其次，即使我们确实将pm_3附加到客户，如果在acct_b上的两个直接费用之间经过一段时间， pm_3可能已经过时。 您应该将平台上的 PaymentMethod 视为规范的。 如果客户想要更新他们存档的 PaymentMethod（例如为其卡提供新的到期日期），您将使用这些更改更新平台上的 PaymentMethod。 这意味着pm_3在下一次直接收费时不会是最新的，除非您费心手动跟踪和更新pm_1的每个克隆副本。

最好的方法是在每次交易时再次从平台克隆规范的 PaymentMethod。 这将始终提供最新的副本，并避免需要管理和更新各种连接帐户上的可重用 PaymentMethod 对象。

其他注意事项

关于克隆 PaymentMethods，还有一些其他事项需要注意。

首先，重要的是要了解克隆的 PaymentMethod 是独立的、完全独立的对象，具有自己的 ID，并且它们与平台上的原始 PaymentMethod 没有链接。 没有任何类型的同步，因此如果您在平台上更新 PaymentMethod，您已经创建的克隆不会受到影响。

其次，只能克隆某些类型的 PaymentMethod 对象，例如卡和 ACH 借记 PaymentMethods。 不能克隆其他对象，例如发票或客户。 如果您想将像客户这样的对象复制到连接的帐户，您需要手动执行此操作。

第三，如果您想使用克隆的 PaymentMethod 进行定期计费，例如使用订阅，您必须将其附加到已连接帐户上的客户，以便可以重复使用。

这就是克隆多个账户的直接费用！ 在您的平台上拥有可重复使用的 PaymentMethod 后，您可以将其无限次克隆到无限数量的连接帐户，以实现您梦想中的直接收费工作流程！



原文:
Transcript

Welcome to another TSE Single Slider!

This one's all about direct charges on multiple accounts with cloning. I'll explain how to collect payment details from a customer once and use them for multiple direct charges on multiple connected accounts.

Getting set up on the platform

Let's start on the platform, where we either create or use an existing Customer. We'll create a SetupIntent associated with this Customer, and when we confirm the SetupIntent client-side we get a PaymentMethod that's been set up for future use. When confirmed successfully, SetupIntents automatically attach the associated PaymentMethod to the associated Customer, which allows the PaymentMethod to be reused.

One quick note before we proceed: for this slide I'm using example object IDs with sequential numbers (e.g., pm_1, pm_2, and so on). Usually I use random example IDs to approximate the random IDs Stripe will generate, but using sequential IDs for this slide will make things easier to understand. Just remember, when you're actually using Stripe, the IDs generated for these objects will be random, and none of them will be sequential or related in any way.

Send in the clones

Okay, now that we have a PaymentMethod set up for future use on the platform, we can clone it to a connected account. Let's start with connected account acct_a, where we want to make our first direct charge.

To clone the PaymentMethod to this connected account we need to make a POST request to /v1/payment_methods. We set the value of the Stripe-Account header to the connected account's ID, and in the body of the request we'll specify the ID of the PaymentMethod we want to clone and, because that PaymentMethod is attached to a Customer, we also need to specify the Customer ID.

When this API request succeeds, a new PaymentMethod object on the connected account is created. Like pm_1 on the platform, this new PaymentMethod (pm_2) represents the same payment information, but other than that it's an entirely new and independent object.

We can now use this cloned PaymentMethod to create a direct charge on the connected account by creating and confirming a PaymentIntent!

Ensure proper set up

Note the cloned PaymentMethod on the connected account inherited the setup for future use performed by the SetupIntent on the platform. This means there's no need to set it up again on the connected account.

One thing to keep in mind here are the countries of the platform and connected accounts. You may need to use on_behalf_of on the SetupIntent to properly set up the PaymentMethod for use with your connected accounts. For example, if your platform account is in the United States, where 3D Secure rarely or never happens, the setup performed by the SetupIntent will likely not prompt for 3D Secure authentication, and will thus be insufficient if you want to make a direct charge on a European connected account, where SCA is enforced.

If you're going to create direct charges in a country that differs from your platform account, you can use on_behalf_of on the SetupIntent to specify a connected account in the same country where the charges will be made to ensure the proper setup is performed.

Second connected account, more direct charges

Now let's look at a second connected account, acct_b. What if we also want to use the same PaymentMethod from the platform to create a direct charge on this other account? It works basically the same way, starting with the same API request to clone the PaymentMethod, with the only change being the account ID we specify in the Stripe-Account header.

Once this API request succeeds we have another new cloned PaymentMethod (pm_3) on acct_b. This PaymentMethod has also inherited the setup from the platform, so we can then create a direct charge the same way.

Let's imagine now that we want to create a second direct charge on acct_b. You might be thinking we can reuse pm_3 for that, but there are two reasons that won't work.

First, we're not attaching the cloned PaymentMethods on the connected accounts to Customers (which enables reuse), which means they can only be used once, and we've already used pm_3 for a direct charge.

Second, even if we did attach pm_3 to a Customer, if some time passed between the two direct charges on acct_b, pm_3 might be out of date. You should think of the PaymentMethod on the platform as the canonical one. If the Customer wants to update their PaymentMethod on file (such as providing a new expiration date for their card) you would update the PaymentMethod on the platform with those changes. That means pm_3 wouldn't be up to date for the next direct charge unless you went to the trouble of keeping track of and updating every cloned copy of pm_1 manually.

The best approach is to clone the canonical PaymentMethod from the platform again, at the time of each transaction. This will always provide an up-to-date copy and avoid the need to manage and update reusable PaymentMethod objects on various connected accounts.

Other things to note

There are a few other things to note about cloning PaymentMethods.

First, it's important to understand that cloned PaymentMethods are separate, completely independent objects with their own IDs, and they have no link back to the original PaymentMethod on the platform. There's no synchronization of any kind, so if you update the PaymentMethod on the platform the clones you've already made are not affected.

Second, only certain types of PaymentMethod objects can be cloned, such as cards and ACH debit PaymentMethods. No other objects, like Invoices or Customers, can be cloned. If you want to copy an object like a Customer to a connected account you need to do so manually.

Third, if you want to use a cloned PaymentMethod for recurring Billing, such as with a Subscription, you must attach it to a Customer on the connected account so it can be reused.

So that's direct charges on multiple accounts with cloning! Once you have a reusable PaymentMethod on your platform you can clone it an unlimited number of times to an unlimited number of connected accounts in order to enable the direct charge workflow of your dreams!

Items.Title: 这个新的 GIT 推送配置将为您节省很多挫折！ | This new GIT push config will save you lot of frustration!
Items.Description:
如果您有 59 秒，特别是您还在使用移动设备，您可能会喜欢观看 YouTube #Shorts 视频。

创建和推送新分支时，您将收到此错误：

 fatal: The current branch feature/my-cool-branch has no upstream branch. To push the current branch and set the remote as upstream, use git push --set-upstream origin feature/my-cool-branch

从 2.37 版本开始，git 引入了一个新的配置，称为push.autoSetupRemote ，它涵盖了这种情况。

从官方文档：

如果设置为“true”，则当当前分支不存在上游跟踪时，默认推送时假定 --set-upstream； 此选项对 push.default 选项 simple、upstream 和 current 生效。 如果默认情况下您希望将新分支推送到默认远程（如 push.default=current 的行为）并且您还希望设置上游跟踪，这很有用。 最有可能从此选项中受益的工作流程是简单的中央工作流程，其中所有分支都应在远程具有相同的名称。

首先，通过运行确保您使用的是 2.37 或更高版本

您可以从https://git-scm.com/或通过命令行下载最新版本，例如在 mac 上使用homebrew ：

现在您已经准备就绪，只需运行以下命令：

 git config --global push.autoSetupRemote true

它将在您的全局 git 配置文件中将值true设置为push.autoSetupRemote 。

使用该设置，所有首次推送新分支将自动设置默认上游。

要现场观看，您可以观看这个简短的 YouTube 视频。

感谢您阅读我的帖子，希望您觉得它有趣！ 有新文章发布时，请随时关注我获得通知；）

您也可以在 GitHub 或 Twitter 上关注我，您可以直接在我的 GitHub 个人资料上找到链接！

原文:

If you have 59 seconds and in particular you're also on mobile, you might enjoy watching the YouTube #Shorts video.

When creating and pushing a new branch, you will get this error:

fatal: The current branch feature/my-cool-branch has no upstream branch.
To push the current branch and set the remote as upstream, use git push --set-upstream origin feature/my-cool-branch

Since version 2.37 git has introduced a new config, called push.autoSetupRemote that covers this case.

From the official documentation:

If set to "true" assume --set-upstream on default push when no upstream tracking exists for the current branch; this option takes effect with push.default options simple, upstream, and current. It is useful if by default you want new branches to be pushed to the default remote (like the behavior of push.default=current) and you also want the upstream tracking to be set. Workflows most likely to benefit from this option are simple central workflows where all branches are expected to have the same name on the remote.

First of all, make sure you're on version 2.37 or higher by just running

You can download the latest version from https://git-scm.com/ or via command line, for example with a mac using homebrew:

Now that you're all set, just run this command:

git config --global push.autoSetupRemote true

It will set in your global git configuration file the value true to push.autoSetupRemote.

With that set, all first-time push on new branches will automatically set the default upstream.

To see it live, you can have a look at this short YouTube video.

Items.Title: Appwrite OSS 基金赞助商 InvenTree | Appwrite OSS Fund Sponsors InvenTree
Items.Description:
用于Appwrite的haimantika mitra

发表于嗨读者👋，欢迎您回到“Appwrite OSS 基金”系列，我们在这里庆祝开源维护者。 作为一家开源公司，我们希望回馈社区并帮助尽可能多的人。

听听我们的创始人兼首席执行官怎么说 - Appwrite 故事：

经过委员会的仔细考虑，我们很高兴地宣布第三个项目：<<<< 一点点支持就大有帮助，带着这个想法，Appwrite 推出了“OSS Fund”。

它旨在提供完整的“端到端”产品生命周期管理，从原材料采购、制造和组装、产品测试和一致性到客户交付。

组织、记录和定位大量零件的库存水平

  • 使用物料清单 (BOM) 管理复杂的零件关系
  • 提供原材料和零部件的全面供应链信息
  • 协助组装商品的制造和调试
  • 通过采购和销售订单跟踪进出货物
  • 在整个制造生命周期中为项目提供可追溯性

    • 他与妻子和女儿住在澳大利亚的塔斯马尼亚。 在他作为机电一体化工程师的“日常工作”中，我在航空航天工业工作，设计电子和嵌入式系统。

    他将“业余”时间用于开发软件，为开源项目（例如 KiCad）做出贡献。 他还发现自己在 InvenTree 上投入了大量时间，这是他真正可以称之为自己的第一个开源项目！

    当他可以将自己从屏幕上拉开时，他喜欢骑山地自行车和丛林徒步。

    在高科技行业的一家小企业工作 Oliver 努力寻找一种“完美契合”特定业务需求的库存管理系统。 他相信其他小型企业将从该软件中受益，尤其是在设计时考虑到了可扩展性。 每个小型企业都有不同的需求，大多数现有的商业软件解决方案都没有提供任何“修补”软件以使其满足特定需求的能力。 这是Inventree的开始。

    最初，InvenTree 的开发是一个个人项目，他认为这是一个有趣的挑战。 最重要的是，这是一个学习一些新的软件技术和范例的机会，而且主要是一种爱好。 该项目的初始范围非常小，几个月后它已发展成为我工作地点有用的目标工具。

    在寻找库存管理工具时，Oliver 知道他想使用开源的东西。 他最初的意图是找到一个最适合我要求的开源项目，并根据需要贡献回额外的功能。 然而，经过广泛的搜索，我发现没有合适的现有解决方案。

    该项目的范围迅速扩大。 很快它就变得更像是一个“通用”软件，此时奥利弗认为开源它并让其他人从他的努力中受益会很有用。

    从那时起，随着越来越多的人找到新的、令人兴奋的方式来使用它，该项目迅速扩展！

    他学到了很多东西，并在此过程中遇到了一些帮助发展和改进项目的优秀人士。
    听到 InvenTree 在全球范围内使用的多种不同方式非常令人鼓舞，我希望我们能够继续扩大该项目充满活力的用户群！

    如果这个故事引起了您或您的朋友的共鸣，请告诉他们 OSS 基金，因为申请仍在开放中：

    如有问题，请通过以下方式联系我们：

    一旦暂停，appwrite 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

    原文:

    haimantika mitra for Appwrite

    Posted on Aug 2

    Hi readers 👋, welcoming you back to the "Appwrite OSS Fund" series, where we celebrate open-source maintainers. 🎉

    🤔 What Is OSS Fund?

    On the 4th of May, the Appwrite team launched the OSS Fund, an initiative to support open-source project maintainers. Being an open-source company, we wanted to give back to the community and help as many people as we can.

    The OSS Fund is an initiative that is very close to our heart. Hear what our Founder and CEO has to say - The Appwrite Story:

    Appwrite provides web and mobile developers with a set of easy-to-use and integrate REST APIs to manage their core backend needs.

    After careful considerations from the committee we are thrilled to announce the third project: <<<<>>>>>>

    A little support goes a long way, with this thought in mind, Appwrite launched "OSS Fund".

    📢 Excited to announce @inventreedb to be the third project we sponsor!

    💸 OSS Fund is still accepting applications, share it with your maintainer friends - appwrite.io/oss-fund


    🤔 What Is InvenTree?

    InvenTree provides a wide range of features and functionality in the “inventory management / stock control” space. It is intended to provide complete “end to end” product lifecycle management, from acquisition of raw materials, manufacturing and assembly, product testing and conformance, to customer delivery.

    Some of the key functionalities of InvenTree include:

    • Organize, record and locate stock levels for large volumes of parts
    • Manage complex part relationships with Bills of Material (BOMs)
    • Provide comprehensive supply chain information for raw materials and components
    • Aid in the manufacture and commissioning of assembled goods
    • Track incoming and outgoing goods via purchase and sales orders
    • Provide traceability for items throughout the entire manufacturing lifecycle

    InvenTree also has a keen focus on extensibility, and provides a rich “plugin” architecture allowing for customization by end users and developers.

    🤝 Meet The Maintainer

    Oliver Walters is the person behind the computer screen, building the awesome InvenTree. He lives in Tasmania, Australia with his wife and daughters. In his “day job” as a Mechatronics Engineer I work in the aerospace industry, designing electronics and embedded systems.

    He spends his "spare" time developing software, contributing to open-source projects (such as KiCad). He also finds himself sinking a lot of time into InvenTree, which is the first open source project he can really call his own!

    When he can pull myself away from a screen, he enjoys mountain biking and bushwalking.

    💡 How Did The Idea Of InvenTree Come Up?

    Workng in a small business in a highly technical industry Oliver struggled to find an inventory management system which was a “perfect fit” for the specific business requirements. He believed that other small businesses would benefit from the software, especially as it was designed with extensibility in mind. Every small business has different needs, and most existing commercial software solutions did not offer any ability to “tinker” with the software to make it fit a particular need. This was the beginning of Inventree.

    🚘 The Journey So Far

    Initially the development of InvenTree was a personal project which he took as an interesting challenge. More than anything else it was an opportunity to learn some new software technologies and paradigms, and was primarily a hobby. The initial scope of the project was quite small, and after a few months it had developed into a useful targeted tool at my place of work.

    Looking for an inventory management tool, Oliver knew he wanted to use something that was open source. His initial intent was to find an open source project which mostly suited my requirements, and contribute back additional features as required. However after an extensive search there were no existing solution which I found suitable.

    The scope of the project quickly ballooned. It was soon at the point where it was becoming more of a “general purpose” piece of software, at which point Oliver thought it would be useful to open source it and allow others to benefit from his efforts.

    Since then, the project has rapidly expanded as more people find new and exciting ways to make use of it!

    🗒️ Ending Notes

    Oliver says, open sourcing the InvenTree project has been a very large undertaking, but has been very rewarding for him. He has learned a great deal, and have met some wonderful people in the process who have helped grow and improve the project.
    Hearing about the many and varied ways in which InvenTree is being used worldwide is extremely encouraging, and I hope that we can continue to grow the project’s vibrant user base!

    If this story resonates with you or your friend, tell them about OSS Fund, as applications are still open:

    Appwrite provides web and mobile developers with a set of easy-to-use and integrate REST APIs to manage their core backend needs.

    For questions, reach out to us on:

原文:

Carlos Cholula

Posted on Aug 2

The HTML element is a mechanism for holding HTML that is not to be rendered immediately when a page is loaded but may be instantiated subsequently during runtime using JavaScript.

Think of a template as a content fragment that is being stored for subsequent use in the document. While the parser does process the contents of the element while loading the page, it does so only to ensure that those contents are valid; the element's contents are not rendered, however.

Example

HTML

<template id="elementTemplate"> <h1>Title Content</h1> <p>Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit</p>

</template>

JS

var template = document.querySelector("#elementTemplate");
document.querySelector(".name__class").appendChild(template.content);

Items.Title: 了解 Javascript 中的 .map()、.filter() 和 .reduce() 数组方法。 | Understanding the .map(), .filter() and .reduce() array methods in Javascript.
Items.Description:
数组是 Javascript 中最常用的数据结构之一，从在我们的代码中存储基本数据到从 API 调用中获取结果。 由于 Javascript 开发人员一直在使用数组并对它们执行某种形式的操作。

在本文中，我们将详细了解这些数组方法的工作原理以及我们如何使用它们来执行一些基本操作。

概述

首先简要看一下这些数组方法的作用。

这些数组方法是高阶函数，这意味着它们将回调函数作为参数。

使用 .map() 方法

let numbers = [ 24 , 11 , 8 , 13 ] const double = numbers . map ( number => score * 2 ) console . log ( newScores ) // outputs [ 48, 22, 16, 26 ]

当我们运行上面的代码时。 请注意， .map()方法将始终返回与原始数组长度相同的数组。

在本教程的其余部分，我们将使用一组对象，其中包含一组员工的姓名、职业和分数。

 const employees = [ { name : " Peter " , score : 25 , occupation : " Engineer " }, { name : " Judy " , score : 30 , occupation : " Banker " }, { name : " Jarvis " , score : 40 , occupation : " Doctor " }, { name : " Paul " , score : 65 , occupation : " Engineer " } ]

使用.map()方法，我们可以将所有员工的姓名保存到一个新数组中。

 const names = employees . map ( person => person . name ) console . log ( names ) //returns ["Peter","Judy","Jarvis","Paul"]

更多回调参数

索引返回迭代中当前元素的索引

该数组返回完整的原始数组。

我个人还没有看到数组参数的任何用例。 如果你看过请在下方评论。

当我们运行上面的代码时， .filter()方法循环遍历 numbers 数组，并检查当前数字是否大于 10，如果为 true，则将数字添加到新数组中。

使用 .filter() 方法

我们可以使用 .filter() 方法从上面的数据中获取工程师员工的数组。

 const allEngineers = people . filter ( person => person . occupation === " Engineer " ) console . log ( allEngineers ) //Outputs [ { name : " Peter " , score : 25 , occupation : " Engineer " }, { name : " Paul " , score : 65 , occupation : " Engineer " } ]

可选回调参数

它评估数组中的所有元素以返回单个值。

此“累加器”参数用于减速器功能。

使用 .reduce 方法获取数字的总和。

 let numbers = [ 24 , 11 , 8 , 13 ] const sum = numbers . reduce (( sum , number ) => { return sum + score }, 0 ) console . log ( sum ) // outputs 56

当上面的代码运行.reduce()方法时，首先将sum的值设置为作为初始值提供的0 ，然后循环遍历数组，在每次迭代时，它会将number加到sum的值上，并在最后一次之后element 返回sum ，在我们的例子中是 56。

虽然初始值可能是可选的，但始终建议指定它，以避免在空数组的情况下出现TypeError 。 我们收到下面的错误消息。

未捕获的类型错误：减少没有初始值的空数组

链接方法

使用这些数组方法的优点之一是能够链接它们。 由于与.forEach()方法不同，这些数组方法返回一个新数组，我们可以将它们链接起来以获得一个组合方法。

链接是组合数组方法以对它们执行操作并获得最终返回值。

使用链式我们可以获得得分在 35 分以上的员工的姓名。

 const passed = people . filter ( person => person . score > 25 ) . map ( person => person . name ) console . log ( passed ) // outputs ["Judy","Jarvis","Paul"]

在上面的代码中，.filter() 操作首先在数组上执行，该数组返回一个分数高于 35 的员工数组，然后 .map() 方法返回他们的姓名作为操作的最终值。

我们也可以只得到工程师分数的总和。

 const engineersScore = people . filter ( person => person . occupation === " Engineer " ) //return an array of engineers only . map ( person => person . score ) //returns the array of engineers score . reduce (( sum , element ) => sum + element ) //sums up the score. console . log ( engineersScore ) //outputs 90

结论

掌握新概念的最佳方法是使用它们。 尽可能尝试使用这些方法代替.forEach() 。

如果您有任何反馈，请随时在下面发表评论，并与其他 Javascript 开发人员分享。

让我们在Twitter 上联系

我使用的资源

MDN MDN



原文:

Arrays are one of the most used data structures in Javascript, from storing basic data in our code to getting results from API calls. As Javascript developers were always working with arrays and carrying out some form of operation on them.

The .map(), .reduce() and .filter() are some of the lesser-known array methods released in ES6, that uses a more functional and cleaner approach to carry out array operations.

In this article, we’ll be taking a detailed look at how these array methods work and how we can use them to carry out some basic operations.

Overview

First a brief look at what these array methods do.

The .map() method returns a new array where each element have gone through an operation of some kind.

The .filter() method returns a new array containing only array elements that passed a specific condition.

The .reduce() method reduces all the elements in the original to a single value.

These array methods are Higher Order Functions, meaning they take callback functions as parameters.

The .map() method

The .map() method returns a new array that contains the result of applying an operation on each of the original array elements.

The .map method accepts a callback function as a parameter and an important parameter for the callback function is the element parameter, the element parameter is processed by the callback function and returned an an element to the new array.

Syntax for the .map() method

Array.map(function (element) { //operation on element
}) //using arrow function
Array.map(element => { //operation on element
})

Using the .map() method 

let numbers = [24, 11, 8, 13] const double = numbers.map(number=> score * 2)
console.log(newScores) // outputs [ 48, 22, 16, 26 ]

When we run the code above. The .map() function loops through the numbers array, assigns the current element in iteration as number, carries out the operation number * 2, and then finally inserts this new value to the new array.

Note that the .map() method will always return an array of the same length as the original array.

For the rest of this tutorial, we'll be working with an array of objects, that contains the name, occupation and score of a group of employees.

const employees = [ { name: "Peter", score: 25, occupation: "Engineer" }, { name: "Judy", score: 30, occupation: "Banker" }, { name: "Jarvis", score: 40, occupation: "Doctor" }, { name: "Paul", score: 65, occupation: "Engineer" }
]

The .map() method can also be used to extract specific data from an array
With the .map() method, we can get the names of all the employees to a new array.

const names = employees.map(person => person.name)
console.log(names) //returns ["Peter","Judy","Jarvis","Paul"]

More callback parameters

The .map() callback function can take more parameters that you might find useful when carrying out array operations. 

Array.map((element, index, array) {operation})

The element parameter is used to access the current element in iteration.

The index returns the index of the current element in iteration

the array returns the complete original array.

I personally haven't seen any use case for the array parameter. If you have seen please comment below.

The .filter() Method

The .filter() returns a new array containing only elements that passed the condition passed in the callback function.

Syntax for the .filter() method

Array.filter(element => { //condition
})

let numbers = [24, 11, 8, 13] const filter = numbers.filter(number => number > 10)
console.log(filter) // output [24,11,13]

When we run the code above the .filter() method loops through the numbers array, and checks if the current number is greater than 10, if true the number is added to the new array.

Using the .filter() method

We can use the .filter() method to get the array of employees who are engineers from our data above.

const allEngineers = people.filter(person => person.occupation === "Engineer")
console.log(allEngineers)
//Outputs  [ { name: "Peter", score: 25, occupation: "Engineer" },
{ name: "Paul", score: 65, occupation: "Engineer" }
]

Optional callback parameters

The .filter() callback function just like the .map() can also take more than just the element parameter as an argument and they also serve the same purpose.

Array.filter((element, index, array) {condition})

The .reduce() method.

The .reduce() method is used to combine all the elements in an array to get a value. It evaluates all of the elements in the array to return a single value.

The .reduce() method is a little more complicated than the other two mentioned above because its callback function requires a specific parameter known as the “accumulator” which holds the total value during the operation and returns it after the operation is completed. This "accumulator" parameter is used in the reducer function.

Syntax for .reduce() method

Array.reduce((accumulator, element) { //reducer
}, initial value)

Using the .reduce method to get the sum of numbers.

let numbers= [24, 11, 8, 13] const sum = numbers.reduce((sum, number) => { return sum + score
}, 0) console.log(sum) // outputs 56

When the code above runs the .reduce() method first set the value of sum to 0 which was provided as the initial value and then loops through the array, on every iteration, it adds number to the value of sum, and after the last element returns sum which in our case is 56.

While the initial value might be optional it's always advisable to specify it, to avoid getting a TypeError in the case of an empty array. We get the error message below.

Uncaught TypeError: Reduce of empty array with no initial value

Chaining Methods

One of the pros of using these array methods is the ability to chain them. Since unlike the .forEach() method these array methods return a new array, we can chain them to get a combo method.

Chaining is combining array methods to perform operations on them and get a final return value.

Using chaining we can get the names of employees who scored above 35.

const passed= people.filter(person => person.score > 25) .map(person => person.name) console.log(passed) // outputs ["Judy","Jarvis","Paul"]

In the code above the .filter() operation is carried out first on the array which returns an array of employees whose score is above 35 then the .map() method returns their name as the final value of the operation.

We can also get the sum of the scores of engineers only.

const engineersScore= people.filter(person => person.occupation === "Engineer") //return an array of engineers only .map(person => person.score) //returns the array of engineers score .reduce((sum, element) => sum + element) //sums up the score. console.log(engineersScore)
//outputs 90

Conclusion

The best way to master new concepts is by using them. Try using these methods in place of the .forEach() when possible.

Feel free to comment below if you have any feedback and please share with other Javascript developers.

Let's Connect on Twitter

Resource I used

Items.Title: 我们发布了一个新的 Next.js 电子商务店面，其中包含与 PayPal、MeiliSearch、Stripe 等的现成集成！ | We Released a New Next.js Ecommerce Storefront with Ready Integrations to PayPal, MeiliSearch, Stripe, and more!
Items.Description:
上个月，我们在Medusa的团队构建了一个新的高级 Next.js 店面，可以与 Medusa 服务器一起使用。 如果对其他美杜莎入门者感兴趣，请随时查看Medusa.Express或我们的Gatsby 入门者。

这个新的 Next.js 店面提供了时尚的设计、必要的电子商务功能以及与第三方服务和工具的强大集成，以便在您的店面中开箱即用地使用支付提供商和搜索引擎。

阅读下面的更多内容，了解这个新店面提供的内容。 您可以查看GitHub 存储库以开始使用店面！

搜索引擎与 Meil​​iSearch 或 Algolia 的集成

该店面已与Meil​​iSearch和Algolia集成。 这使客户可以轻松找到他们需要的产品。

只需在您的 Medusa 服务器上安装Meil​​iSearch或Algolia插件即可。 然后，可以在store.config.json中开启search功能：

 { "features" : { "search" : true }
}

这就是支持 Meil​​iSearch 所需的全部内容，对于 Algolia，需要对代码进行额外的更改，可以在文档中找到。

支付提供商与 PayPal 和 Stripe 的集成

如果您在 Medusa 服务器上安装了Stripe和PayPal插件，Next.js 店面将在结账时自动为它们显示必要的 UI！ 您可以同时使用这两个支付提供商，也可以只使用其中一个。

您需要做的就是在店面的环境变量中为 Stripe 和 PayPal 添加必要的 API 密钥：

 NEXT_PUBLIC_STRIPE_KEY = <YOUR_PUBLISHABLE_KEY> NEXT_PUBLIC_PAYPAL_CLIENT_ID = <YOUR_CLIENT_ID>

然后，如果您在 Medusa 服务器中启用了 Stripe，您将在结帐时看到卡片表格。

如果您在 Medusa 服务器上启用了 PayPal，您将在结帐时看到 PayPal 按钮，以便轻松使用 PayPal 付款。

电子商务功能

该店面配备了基本的电子商务功能，可为客户提供出色的用户体验。

产品和系列

店面包括无缝导航以查看产品和集合。 您还可以按集合过滤产品。

产品详情页面以时尚的滚动设计展示了产品的所有重要信息。 客户可以轻松选择产品变体并将其添加到购物车。

购物车和完整结帐功能

客户可以随时查看他们的购物车并对其进行编辑。 然后，客户可以通过单页结账流程下订单。

客户账户

客户可以注册或登录以受益于个人资料功能，例如保存的地址、订单历史记录等。

完全可定制

整个店面是完全可定制的！ 您可以更改src目录下所有可用的页面、组件和样式。

这使开发人员可以自由地重新设计店面、添加更多功能以及集成更多他们认为合适的第三方服务。

开始使用 Next.js 店面

运行 Next.js Storefront 需要安装并运行 Medusa 服务器。 按照快速入门指南，您可以在几分钟内开始使用美杜莎。

然后，安装 Next.js Storefront 可以通过 3 个步骤完成：

  1. 使用店面的起始模板创建 Next.js 项目：
 npx create-next-app -e https://github.com/medusajs/nextjs-starter-medusa my-medusa-storefront
  1. 切换到新创建的目录my-medusa-storefront并重命名模板环境变量文件以在开发中使用环境变量：
 mv .env.template .env.local
  1. 确保 Medusa 服务器正在运行，然后运行本地 Next.js 服务器：

如果您需要启用/禁用上述任何集成，请查看文档以了解如何操作。

你怎么看？

你喜欢这个新的 Next.js 店面吗？ 您对如何改进有任何反馈吗？ 请在留言中让我们知道你的想法！

如果您有任何与美杜莎相关的问题或疑问，请随时通过Discord与美杜莎团队联系。



原文:

Last month, our team at Medusa built a new advanced Next.js storefront that can be used with a Medusa server. If interested in other Medusa starters, feel free to check out Medusa.Express or our Gatsby starter.

This new Next.js storefront provides a sleek design, necessary ecommerce features, and powerful integrations with third-party services and tools to use payment providers and search engines in your storefront out-of-the-box.

Read more below to learn about what this new storefront provides. You can check out the GitHub repository to get started with the storefront!

Search Engine Integration with MeiliSearch or Algolia

This storefront comes with ready integration with MeiliSearch and Algolia. This allows customers to find the product they need easily.

All that is required is to install the MeiliSearch or Algolia plugin on your Medusa server. Then, the search feature can be turned on in store.config.json:

{ "features": { "search": true }
}

That’s all you need to support MeiliSearch, for Algolia, an additional change is required to make in the code which can be found in the documentation.

Payment Provider Integrations with PayPal and Stripe

If you have the Stripe and PayPal plugins installed on your Medusa server, the Next.js storefront will show the necessary UI for them during checkout automatically! You can use both of the payment providers or only one of them.

All that you need to do is add the necessary API keys for Stripe and PayPal in your storefront’s environment variables:

NEXT_PUBLIC_STRIPE_KEY=<YOUR_PUBLISHABLE_KEY>
NEXT_PUBLIC_PAYPAL_CLIENT_ID=<YOUR_CLIENT_ID>

Then, if you have Stripe enabled in your Medusa server, you’ll see the card form during checkout.

If you have PayPal enabled on your Medusa Server, you’ll see the PayPal button on checkout to easily pay with PayPal.

Ecommerce Features

This storefront comes fully packed with essential ecommerce features to provide a great user experience to customers.

Products and Collections

The storefront includes seamless navigation to view both products and collections. You can also filter products by collection.

The detail page of products showcases all the product’s important information with a sleek scroll design. Customers can easily choose the product variants and add them to the cart.

Cart and Full Checkout Features

Customers can view their carts and make edits to them at any point. Then, customers can go through the one-page checkout flow to place their order.

Customer Accounts

Customers can sign up or log in to benefit from profile features such as saved addresses, order history, and more.

Completely Customizable

The entire storefront is completely customizable! You can make changes to the pages, components, and styles all available under the src directory.

This gives developers the freedom to redesign the storefront, add more features, and integrate more third-party services as they see fit.

Get Started with the Next.js Storefront

Running the Next.js Storefront requires an installed and running Medusa server. You can get started with Medusa in minutes by following the quickstart guide.

Then, installing the Next.js Storefront can be done in 3 steps:

  1. Create a Next.js project using the storefront’s starter template: 
npx create-next-app -e https://github.com/medusajs/nextjs-starter-medusa my-medusa-storefront
  1. Change to the newly created directory my-medusa-storefront and rename the template environment variable file to use environment variables in development: 
mv .env.template .env.local
  1. Make sure the Medusa server is running, then run the local Next.js server:

If you need to enable/disable any of the integrations mentioned above, check out the documentation to learn how to do it.

What do you think?

Do you like this new Next.js storefront? Do you have any feedback on how it can be improved? Let us know what you think in the comments!

Should you have any issues or questions related to Medusa, then feel free to reach out to the Medusa team via Discord.

原文:

Toda pessoa desenvolvedora de software deve se preocupar com a confiabilidade do sistema, essa não é uma tarefa apenas do time de SREs.

SRE, sigla em inglês para Site Reliability Engineering ou "Engenharia de Confiabilidade de Sites", é uma abordagem da engenharia de software às operações de TI. As equipes de SRE usam software como uma ferramenta para gerenciar sistemas, solucionar problemas e automatizar tarefas operacionais.

Se você está desenvolvendo software, então você tem interesse em garantir que esse software seja o mais confiável ​​possível.

Aqui está um tópico com sugestões e observações sobre este tópico:

O que é confiabilidade?

A confiabilidade do sistema é a probabilidade de um sistema desempenhar suas funções requeridas por um tempo e sob condições especificadas.

É importante porque determina a probabilidade do sistema atender às necessidades de seus usuários.

Planejar durante a fase de projeto é muito mais fácil do que deixar para depois.

A arquitetura para confiabilidade na nuvem envolve ter consciência das deficiências dos sistemas distribuídos.

Confiabilidade é um "subconjunto" de disponibilidade, que pode ser interpretado como a porcentagem de tempo em que o serviço está ativo.

Se sua disponibilidade for baixa e seu sistema estiver frequentemente inativo, os usuários não poderão usar seu produto e vão em breve migrar para outro sistema.

  • A confiabilidade não é responsabilidade exclusiva do engenheiro de SRE, mas de todos na organização.

A confiabilidade é crucial porque o tempo de atividade afeta diretamente o resultado final.

O tempo de inatividade significa perda de produtividade, o que resulta em perda de receita.

Todos precisam estar cientes e assumir a responsabilidade pela confiabilidade do sistema para garantir que ele esteja disponível quando necessário.

Como os diferentes papéis contribuem:

SRE - Ajudam a identificar riscos e manter o sistema funcionando. Eles garantem que existam procedimentos de backup em caso de falhas.

Produto - Eles defendem as necessidades dos usuários e garantem que o sistema atenda às suas expectativas.

Engenheiros - Eles projetam e implementam aplicativos resilientes. Eles lidam com erros com facilidade e garantem que os dados sejam armazenados de forma durável para que possam ser recuperados em caso de falha.

Tester - Eles testam o sistema completamente para garantir que ele seja capaz de lidar com toda a carga e tráfego esperados.

  • As organizações devem ser proativas na prevenção de problemas de confiabilidade, em vez de serem reativas ao lidar com eles após a ocorrência.

Melhorar a confiabilidade não é uma atividade única, pois requer uma cultura de aprendizado e aprimoramento contínuos.

Os problemas de confiabilidade geralmente são resultado de:

• Falta de experiência na identificação de riscos de confiabilidade.

• Falta de foco organizacional na confiabilidade do sistema durante as fases do Ciclo de Vida de Desenvolvimento de Software.

• Falta de suporte para criar e manter condições que sustentem a confiabilidade ao longo do tempo.

Isso geralmente leva a falhas não detectadas que só surgem sob condições de carga não testadas.

Este é um lugar difícil de se estar.

  • Adote uma abordagem proativa para lidar com mudanças arriscadas.

Na evolução de um sistema (especialmente grandes mudanças), a gestão de riscos é fundamental.

Isso significa estar ciente dos riscos potenciais associados à mudança e tomar medidas para mitigar esses riscos antes que eles causem problemas.

Muitas vezes, isso significa realizar etapas extras de desenvolvimento para garantir que não haja tempo de inatividade, como migrar usuários sem problemas para evitar inconvenientes.

  • Monitore seu sistema para prevenir problemas e evitar que eles afetem os usuários.

O monitoramento é crucial para detectar problemas desde o início, para que possam ser resolvidos antes que afetem a experiência do cliente..

Também pode ajudar a identificar tendências que podem levar a problemas futuros.

Quando as coisas dão errado, você quer minimizar o impacto em seus clientes.

Investir em observabilidade pode ajudá-lo a identificar erros rapidamente e reduzir o tempo médio de recuperação.

Resumindo:

  1. Aumentar a conscientização sobre confiabilidade e compartilhar responsabilidades.
  2. Compreender as causas comuns de problemas de confiabilidade.
  3. Adotar uma abordagem proativa para lidar com riscos e antecipar falhas.
  4. Monitorar seus sistemas e invista em observabilidade.

Items.Title: 开源、哲学、仇恨和其他随机词 | Open source, philosophy, hate & other random words
Items.Description:
前段时间，我们的团队发布了我们的第一个开源库。 我们完成了第一个生产就绪版本，将其发布在 Reddit 上，并等待开源社区张开双臂欢迎我们。 毕竟，我们正在投入自己的空闲时间来创造一个伟大的产品来帮助他人，而这一切都是免费的。

哦，我们是多么天真……当然，有些人真的很欣赏我们的贡献，但有些人不明白我们在做什么，而奶油蛋糕只是想讨厌。

我认为在当今世界很难把你的工作放在那里。 当您发布代码时，您会感到非常脆弱，有点赤裸。 就像你的灵魂在外面，让每个人都可以看到和批评。 不要误会我的意思，我认为批评非常重要，它是学习和成长的最佳方式之一。

另一方面，完全忽略别人的工作很容易。 它几乎不需要任何时间和成本。 我也很确定有些人非常喜欢它。

那么当你遇到这样的人时，你应该如何反应呢？ 第一个本能是忽略或“大喊”回来，完全驳回所有批评并继续前进。 我决定不这样做，慢慢地呼吸，从 1 数到 10，就像我妈妈过去常说的那样，再读一遍，想想它是从哪里来的。

例如，在其中一种情况下，由于与项目的编码风格存在分歧，评论以“我会远离它”结尾。 剧透：几次来回之后，对话以“祝你好运！”结束。 那个怎么样？

编程的疯狂和美妙之处。 我们认为这是客观的，它是精确的科学，有对错之分。 但事实是，它也是艺术，其中有很大的主观成分。

我意识到我的编码理念是不同的。 尽管我们仍然存在分歧，但得知做完全相同的事情有完全不同的观点是很有趣的，而且我在这个过程中学到了一两件事。

这一切的意义何在？ 我认为开源是惊人的。 出于某种原因，我们有一种疯狂的动力来免费开发东西，只是为了与世界分享并帮助他人。 尽管如此，我认为我们的讨论和批评还有很大的改进空间。 也许我又天真了，但是我仍然相信社区，我们的社区可以做得更好，并希望我们所有人之间有更好的沟通（希望通过啤酒）。

如果您想查看我们的开源库并留下反馈，我们将不胜感激。



原文:

Some time ago our team has released our first open source library to the wild. We finished our first production ready version, posted it on Reddit and waited for the open source community to welcome us with open arms. After all, we are investing our own free time to create a great product to help others, and it's all for FREE.

Oh how naive we were... Of course some truly appreciated our contribution, yet others didn't understand what we are doing, and the crème de la crème just wanted to hate.

I think that in today's world it's hard to put your work out there. When you post your code you feel very vulnerable, a bit naked. It's like your soul is out there for everyone to see and criticize. And don't get me wrong, I think criticism is very important and it's one of the best ways to learn and grow.

On the other hand, it's very easy to completely dismiss someone else's work. It takes almost no time and costs nothing. Also I'm pretty sure some people enjoy it very much.

So when you meet someone like that, how should you react? The first instinct is to ignore or "yell" back, dismiss all criticism completely and move forward. I decided to do otherwise, breathe and count from 1 to 10, slowly, like my mom used to say, read it again, and think where it is coming from.

For example, in one of these scenarios a comment ended with "I would stay away from it" because of a disagreement with the project's coding style. Spoiler: after a few back and forths the conversation ended with "Good luck though!". How about that?

What is crazy and beautiful about programming. We think it is objective, that it is exact science and there is right and wrong. But the truth is, it is also art, and there is a huge subjective component to it.

What I realized is just that my coding philosophy is different. Even though we are still in a disagreement, it was fascinating to learn that there are completely different points of view for doing the exact same thing, and I learned a thing or two in the process.

What is the point of all of this? I think open source is amazing. For some reason we have crazy drive to develop stuff for free just to share it with the world and help others. Nonetheless, I think there is a lot of room for improvement in our discussions and critique. Maybe I'm naive again, however I still believe the community, our community can do better and hope for better communication between us all (and hopefully over a beer).

If you would like to check out our open source library and leave feedback we would appreciate it greatly.

Items.Title: 为什么 NodeJS 在前端？ | Why NodeJS in Front Ends?
Items.Description:
当我开始 Web 开发时，我在 Youtube 上观看（并且仍在观看）Web 开发人员的频道（例如Fireship.io ）。 一直困扰我的一件事是他们在使用前端框架或库时使用 NodeJS。

像大多数开发人员一样，我用谷歌搜索了这些主题。 经过几个小时敲打我的键盘（用手和头🙃），我收集到的东西是：

  1. 前端和后端都使用 NodeJS
  2. 大多数“入门”教程都在讨论使用 Node JS 作为服务器和后端的东西（即：与前端开发完全相反。）

他们都没有回答我为什么在前端环境中使用 NodeJS 的问题。

快进一些 NodeJS 介绍和阅读前端框架的文档，我终于掌握了为什么和如何。 在这里，我想免去其他 Web 开发人员的麻烦。

对 NodeJS 的一点介绍

这是我最好的一句话：“这是没有GUI的浏览器控制台”。 你给它 JS 代码，它就会运行它们。 唯一的工作就是运行JavaScript，同时允许访问浏览器上不常见的其他内容（读/写文件、执行 shell 脚本等）

所以它是一个后端软件？

从技术上讲是的，它位于您的计算机（或服务器）上，而不是浏览器上。

那你到底为什么要用它来开发前端的东西呢？

与往常一样，真相总是有点复杂。

如果你接触 Python 的时间足够长获得缓慢 II 效果它是一个包管理器，这意味着它可以帮助您安装库、卸载它们并跟踪您已安装的内容。

同样，NodeJS 也有 NPM（虽然还有其他的，这个是默认的）。

但它也有前端框架作为包：React、Vue、Svelte 等。

为什么当我们有 CDN 链接时它就在那里？ 我现在应该使用哪一个？

原因如下：

这就是您通常将您喜欢的框架导入您的站点的方式：

 <script src= "https://cdn.js/myframework/doDSF923HkHWEdss==" ></script>

你问这有什么问题？ 好吧，如果您有多个框架，那么 HTML 文件中的标签顺序就变得至关重要。 一些script标签必须放在较高的位置，而必须放在较低的位置。

搞砸了，准备好下午的调试会议😵。

因此，您可以在 JS 文件中使用它而不是 HTML 中的script标签：

 const MyFramework = require ( " myframework " )

我承认，这是一个边缘案例，但下一个肯定会让每个人都受益。

开发工具可以包括：

  • 模块打包器，将所有 JS 代码及其依赖项组合到一个大文件中。 示例包括webpack和browserify 。
  • 其中之一是common-shakeify

和别的...

当然，您可以使用任何语言来编写这些程序，但是解析 JS 代码最好使用 JS 本身来完成。

而要使用 JS，你需要一种在没有浏览器的情况下运行 JS 的方法，因此需要使用 NodeJS。

此外，前端开发人员不必为了制作开发工具😵‍💫而在头脑中强制使用另一种编程语言的语法。 他们明白自己想要什么，所以我们为什么不给他们一点推动力呢？

结束语

所以，我想这就是我要说的。 人们使用 NodeJS 是因为它更容易管理库，同时可以访问大量开发工具。

这些是相同的东西（还有其他......东西），它们的功能是在浏览器之外运行 JS。

归根结底，它们是相同的东西，您可以选择任何您喜欢的东西。 虽然NodeJS更受欢迎。

快乐编码！ 🙌 🙌



原文:

Back when I started web development, I watched (and still watching) web developers' channels on Youtube (such as Fireship.io). One of the things that kept bugging me is the fact that they use NodeJS when working with front end frameworks or library.

Like most developers, I googled the topics. After a few hours of banging my keyboards (both with hands and head 🙃), the things that I gathered are:

  1. NodeJS is a runtime for JS (what?)
  2. You use NodeJS for both front-end and back-end
  3. Most "Getting Started" tutorials talk about using Node JS as server and back-end stuff (i.e: the complete opposite of front-end dev.)

None of them answered my questions of why do you use NodeJS in front end environment.

Fast forward a few NodeJS intros and reading docs of front end frameworks, I finally grasped the why's and how's. And here, I would like to save the hassle of other web developers who are dipping their toes in the water.

A bit-sized intro to NodeJS

Here's my best one-liner for this: "It's the browser's console without GUIs". You give it JS code, and it will run them. NodeJS is actually based on Chrome's V8 JS engine, so it really is an upgraded component of a browser 🤯.

It's only job is to run JavaScript, while giving access to other stuff not commonly done on browser (read/write files, executing shell scripts, etc.)

So it's a back-end software?

Technically yes, it sits on your computer (or servers), not on the browser.

Then why in the world would you develop front-end stuff with that?

As always, the truth is always a tad bit complicated.

Purpose of NodeJS on Front End dev.

If you have been exposed to Python long enough to acquire Slowness II effect, you may have heard of PIP. It's a package manager, meaning that it helps you install libraries, uninstall them, and keep tracks of what you have installed.

Similarly, NodeJS has NPM (though there are others, this one is the default).

NPM have packages that you might expect: Express for back-end, http library to listen to requests, etc.

But it also have front end frameworks as packages: React, Vue, Svelte, etc.

Why it's there when we have CDN links? Which one should I use now?

And here are the reasons:

1. Easier package management

This is how you normally would import your favorite frameworks into your site:

<script src="https://cdn.js/myframework/doDSF923HkHWEdss=="></script>

What's wrong with that, you ask? Well, if you have multiple frameworks, the order of tags inside the HTML file become critical. Some script tags have to be put at a higher order, while have to be placed lower.

Mess this up, and get ready for an afternoon debugging session 😵.

NodeJS uses the CommonJS syntax for exporting and importing "modules" (e.g: importing your frameworks). So instead of script tags in HTML, you use this in your JS file:

const MyFramework = require("myframework")

I do admit, it's kinda an edge case, but the next one would surely benefit everyone.

2. JS for programming development tools

A neat aspect of NodeJS is that it allows building tools that can seamlessly interact with your front end code, because it natively uses JS. Development tools can include:

  • Module bundlers, which combine all your JS code and its dependencies into one massive file. Examples include webpack and browserify.
  • Tree shakers, which "shakes" unused or dead codes from your JS files. One of them is common-shakeify

and others...

Sure, you can use any language under the sun to program these, but parsing JS code is better done with JS itself.

And to use JS, you need a way to run JS without browser, hence NodeJS.

Also, front-end developers don't have to force another programming language's syntax inside their head just to make a dev tool 😵‍💫. They understand what they want, so why don't we give them a little push?

Closing words

So, I guess that's all I have to say about this. TLDR; people use NodeJS because it's easier to manage libraries, while having access to lots of dev tools.

There are certainly other alternatives to NodeJS, like bun and deno. These are the same stuff (with additional... stuff), their function is to run JS outside of your browser.

They are the same stuff at the end of the day, and you can choose whatever you like. Though NodeJS is way more popular.

Happy coding! 🙌

Items.Title: 字符计数器 | Character Counter
Items.Description:
有些人更喜欢 Word Counter 提供的写作细节的简单性，而这正是该程序提供的功能。 它显示字符数和字数，这通常是人们在写作时必须了解的唯一数据。 这也是快速接收所有必要信息的最佳方式。

要确定写作的字数和字符数，只需将文本复制粘贴到工具中，或直接在框中输入。 完成后，在线工具将显示您添加的文本的字数和字符数。 这在许多情况下很有用，但是在为具有最小字符或限制要求的内容编写时特别有用。

单词和字符的限制在 Internet 中很常见。 大多数人都知道 Twitter 上的推文有 140 个字符的限制。但是，字符限制不仅适用于 Twitter。 文本 (SMS) 以及 Yelp 上的评论帖子、Pinterest 上的 Facebook 帖子 Pins、Reddit 标题和评论以及 eBay 描述和标题等等都有限制。 意识到这些限制并在您接近它们时意识到它们，将使您在法律规定的限制内更有创意地表达自己。

家庭作业通常有限制或最低要求。 大学申请也是如此。 遵循这些准则可能是评估和评估本文的重要因素，并将显示您遵循基本准则的能力。 字符计数器可以帮助确保您不会超出限制或低于可能损害此分配的最低要求。

这些数据对作家来说非常有用。 了解字数和字符可以帮助作家了解他们的工作时间，并帮助他们以某种方式展示作品。 如果您为空间不足且空间不足的报纸或杂志撰稿，了解这些数字将有助于作者从空间中榨取最大的信息。

对于那些正在寻找工作的人来说，重要的是要知道简历上的字符数量对于满足你需要在一页上传达的所有内容至关重要。 您可以尝试使用各种字体、尺寸和间距来改变您可以在单个页面上容纳的字符数量，但您需要注意要在页面上容纳的字符数。

字符计数器不仅适用于英语。 对于那些用英语以外的其他语言写作的人来说，它可能对字符数至关重要的人很有用。 日语、韩语、中文等语言可能就是这种情况，其中字符构成了写作的基础。 即使对于那些不会用英语写作的人来说，理解写作的字符数也会有助于写作。



原文:

Character Counter is a 100percent free online calculator of character counts which is easy to use. Some people prefer simplicity over the writing details Word Counter provides, and that's exactly the feature this program provides. It shows characters count and word count which is typically the only data a person must be aware of about their writing. It is also the best way to will receive all the necessary information quickly.

To determine the word count and character number of your writing, just copy-paste your text into the tool, or type straight into the box. Once you're done, the online tool will show both the word and character counts of the text you've added. This is useful in many cases however it is particularly helpful when writing for something with the requirement of a minimum character or limit.

Limits on words and characters are commonplace in the Internet. The one most people are conscious of is the 140-character limit on tweets on Twitter However, the limits on characters don't just apply to Twitter. There are limitations for texts (SMS) as well as review posts on Yelp, facebook postings Pins on Pinterest, Reddit title and comment as well as eBay descriptions and titles and many more. Being aware of these limits and being aware of them as you get closer to them, will allow you to be more creative in your expression within the limits set by law.

There are typically limitations or minimums on homework assignments. Similar is the case when it comes to college applications. Following these guidelines can be a significant factor in the way that this writing is evaluated and evaluated, and will show how well you're capable of following the basic guidelines. Character counters can help ensure that you don't go over the limits or fall short of the minimum requirements which can harm this assignment.

This data can be very useful for writers. Knowing the word count and characters may aid writers in understanding how long they are work, and help them display the writing in a certain manner. If you write for newspapers or magazines in which space is not plentiful and space, knowing these numbers will assist the writer to squeeze maximum information from the space.

For those looking for jobs it is important to know the amount of characters on your resume could be crucial to fit everything you need to convey on one page. You can experiment with various fonts, dimensions and spacing to alter the amount of characters you can fit onto a single page, but you need to be aware of the number you're trying to fit on the page.

Character Counter isn't just for English. It can be useful to those who write in languages other than English where the character count is crucial. This could be the case for languages such as Japanese, Korean, Chinese and many more where characters form the foundation for writing. Even for those not writing in English understanding the characters count of the writing can be helpful to writing.

Items.Title: 什么是加密钱包？ 你应该选择哪一个？ | What are Crypto Wallets? Which One Should You Choose?
Items.Description:
由于采用率不断提高，加密货币不再需要介绍。 越来越多的人开始投资加密货币，旨在充分利用其升值价值，同时通过交易利用其波动性。 在这个快速增长的行业领域，加密钱包发挥着至关重要的作用，因为它们是持有、管理和与加密资产交互的必要条件。

这些加密钱包有很多选择，在加密资产的安全性、密钥管理和用户友好性等方面各有不同。 它们各有优缺点，我们将在本文中讨论，特别是为了加密领域的新手。

那么，什么是加密钱包？
让我们重温一下基础知识。 与您存储现金和卡的常规钱包不同，加密钱包本身并不真正存储任何加密货币。 相反，它们只存储与区块链上可用资产相关的公钥和私钥。 公钥将与各自的私钥相关联，并充当可以接收加密资产的区块链上的地址。 它还表示将资产发送到区块链上其他钱包的地址。

但是，在发送资金时，私钥对于签署和授权任何其他地址的交易至关重要。 因此，简单来说，私钥就像是执行交易的安全密码。

作为用作公钥和私钥的加密哈希的存储，加密钱包的范围可以从一张纸到物理驱动器，再到存储和处理信息以完成交易的复杂算法程序。 通常，使用钱包的便利性越低，它就越安全。 您使用这些钱包的方式和使用方式取决于用户的便利性和他们正在寻找的安全类型。

不同形式的加密钱包
加密钱包有三种主要形式。 硬件钱包、软件和纸钱包。

硬件钱包
硬件钱包是一种专门的、安全的硬件设备，例如存储私钥的 U 盘。 众所周知，这些设备非常安全，因为密钥存储在硬件安全模块 (HSM) 中，除非连接到在线设备进行交易，否则该模块始终保持离线状态。 除了存储在安全芯片上的密钥外，用户还应该能够物理访问设备并了解隐藏私钥的密码，以便从硬件钱包进行任何交易。

在硬件钱包上，用户必须通过多个耗时的步骤进行交易。 这使得它们对于那些进行大量加密交易的加密交易者来说是不切实际的。

软件钱包
这些通常是移动、桌面或基于 Web 的钱包应用程序，在安全功能层下存储私钥。 在这些钱包中，只要安装它的设备连接到互联网，私钥就会保持在线。 软件钱包主要有两种类型，托管型和非托管型。 托管钱包通常由第三方平台运营，这些平台也拥有钱包的私钥。 同时，非托管钱包允许用户完全控制他们钱包的私钥，这使得他们比托管钱包更安全。

软件钱包通常会实施额外的安全功能，例如 2FA 和密码，但它们仍然容易受到可能危及私钥的黑客攻击、漏洞利用和恶意软件攻击的影响。

纸钱包
作为一种安全且技术含量低的替代品，纸质加密钱包是绝对的冷钱包，通过将密钥打印在一张纸上来离线维护密钥。 要访问基金，用户必须输入私钥或在软件钱包应用程序上扫描代表私钥的二维码。 虽然纸质钱包不受在线事件的影响，但它们在现实世界中非常脆弱。 它需要存放在安全的地方并受到保护，因为它们被破坏或被盗的可能性要高得多。 任何可以访问纸质钱包的人都可以访问该地址中存储的所有资金。

选择合适的钱包
选择正确的加密钱包取决于个人的要求。 特定类型的钱包可能无法满足您的所有要求，并且没有规则可以阻止使用不同类型的钱包来管理其资产。

虽然硬件钱包仍然是最安全的选择，但除了多步骤交易执行过程之外，具有额外安全功能和良好钱包处理实践的托管软件钱包紧随其后。 硬件钱包有前期采购成本，它们完全适合那些以保护资金为首要任务的人的要求。 然而，那些频繁进行加密交易的人可能会觉得它们很麻烦。 他们可以找到更合适的托管软件钱包。 在这两种情况下，钱包用户都应该采取必要的步骤来备份他们的私钥或助记词，并将它们存储在安全的地方。

同时，加密货币交易者会更愿意使用交易所和交易平台提供的非托管钱包，因为它们可以实现近乎即时的交易，但代价是私钥无法控制用户。 使用非托管钱包的最佳做法是存储足够的资金以满足即时交易需求，同时将大部分资产保存在托管硬件或软件钱包中，甚至是纸质钱包，只要采取必要的预防措施。

选择钱包时永远不要忘记的一条黄金法则是，只有在相关的私钥得到保护并保持私密的情况下，加密钱包才是安全的。 只有控制钱包的私钥，用户才能完全控制他们的加密货币。

使用 Liminal 保护您的加密资产
作为传统钱包解决方案的替代方案，其量身定制的产品为加密资产提供了最高的安全标准。 使用 Liminal，用户可以通过额外的措施有效地管理他们的资产和钱包密钥，以防止因盗窃或丢失密钥而造成的损失。

借助自动化的多重签名钱包基础设施，Liminal 允许用户创建验证和私钥恢复机制，以确保即使最终丢失私钥也能获得资金。 除了密钥恢复之外，Liminal 还为加密所有者提供继承解决方案，为他们提供一种方式，让他们的亲人在他们去世时能够访问他们的资产。 此外，Liminal Smart Group 钱包还允许多人操作一个钱包。

原文:

Cryptocurrencies need no introduction anymore, thanks to their rising adoption. More and more people have started investing in cryptocurrencies, aiming to get the most out of their appreciating value while leveraging their volatility through trading. In this fast-growing industry segment, crypto wallets play a crucial role as they are a necessity to hold, manage and interact with the crypto assets.

There are many choices when it comes to these crypto wallets, each differing from the other in aspects like the security of crypto assets, key management, and user-friendliness. They have their share of advantages and shortcomings, which we will discuss in this article, especially for the benefit of newcomers to the crypto scene.

So, what are Crypto Wallets?
Let us revisit the basics. Unlike regular wallets where you store cash and cards, crypto wallets don’t really store any cryptocurrencies within themselves. Instead, they just store the public and private keys associated with the assets that are available on the blockchain. The public key will be associated with the respective private key and acts as an address on the blockchain that can receive crypto assets. It also denotes the address from which assets are sent to other wallets on the blockchain.

However, while sending funds the private key is essential to sign and authorize the transaction to any other address. So, in simple words, the private key is quite like a secure password to execute transactions.

As a store for cryptographic hashes that act as public and private keys, a crypto wallet can range from a piece of paper to physical drives to complex algorithmic programs that store and process information to complete transactions. Usually, the lesser the convenience of using a wallet the more secure it is. How and which of these wallets you use depends on the user’s convenience and the kind of security they are looking for.

Different Forms of Crypto Wallets
There are three main forms of crypto wallets. Hardware wallets, Software, and Paper Wallets.

Hardware Wallets
A hardware wallet is a specialized, secure hardware device like a USB stick on which the private key is stored. These devices are known to be very secure because the keys are stored within a hardware security module (HSM) that always remains offline unless connected to an online device to make transactions. Apart from the keys being stored on a secure chip, users should have physical access to the device and have knowledge of the password that obscures the private key to make any transaction from a hardware wallet.

On a hardware wallet, users must navigate through multiple time-consuming steps to make transactions. This makes them impractical for those like crypto traders who conduct high-volume crypto transactions.

Software Wallets
These are generally mobile, desktop, or web-based wallet applications storing private keys under layers of security features. In these wallets, the private keys stay online as long as the device on which it is installed is connected to the internet. There are mainly two types of software wallets, custodial and non-custodial. Custodial wallets are usually operated by third-party platforms that also possess the private keys for the wallet. Meanwhile, a non-custodial wallet allows users to maintain complete control over their wallet’s private keys, which makes them a lot more secure than their custodial counterparts.

Software wallets generally implement additional security features like 2FA and passcodes, yet they remain susceptible to hacking attempts, bug exploits, and malicious software attacks that could compromise the private keys.

Paper Wallets
A safe and low-tech alternative, paper crypto wallets are absolute cold wallets where the keys are maintained offline by printing them on a piece of paper. To access the fund users will have to enter the private key or scan the QR representing the private key on a software wallet application. While paper wallets are secure from online events, they are very vulnerable in the real world. It needs to be stored in a safe place and protected from the elements as the chance of them getting destroyed or stolen is much higher. Anyone who gets access to the paper wallet will gain access to all the funds stored in that address.

Picking the Right Wallet
Choosing the right crypto wallet depends on an individual’s requirement. A particular type of wallet may not meet all of your requirements, and there is no rule that prevents one from using different types of wallets to manage their assets.

While hardware wallets remain the safest bet, except for the multi-step transaction execution process, custodial software wallets with additional security features and good wallet handling practices come a close second. Hardware wallets have an upfront cost of acquisition and they perfectly suit the requirements of those whose primary priority is to safeguard their funds. However, those making frequent crypto transactions may find them cumbersome. They could find custodial software wallets a better fit. In both cases, the wallet user should take the necessary steps to back up their private keys or mnemonics and store them in a secure place.

Meanwhile, crypto traders would be more comfortable using non-custodial wallets offered by exchanges and trading platforms as they enable near-instantaneous transactions, but at the cost of private keys not being in control of the user. The best practice to use non-custodial wallets is to store just enough funds to meet the immediate transaction needs, while safekeeping the majority of the holdings in a custodial hardware or software wallet, or even a paper wallet as long as necessary precautions are taken.

A golden rule to be never forgotten while choosing a wallet is that the crypto wallets are safe only as long as the associated private keys are secured and remain private. The user will have complete control over their crypto funds only if they are in control of their wallet’s private keys.

Secure Your Crypto Assets with Liminal
Liminal provides a digital assets storage and security infrastructure solution for crypto-native businesses. Its tailormade products offer the highest safety standards to crypto assets as an alternative to conventional wallet solutions. With Liminal, users can efficiently manage their assets and wallet keys with additional measures to prevent losses due to theft or lost keys.

With an automated multisig wallet infrastructure, Liminal allows users to create verification and private key recovery mechanisms that ensure access to funds even if they end up losing their private keys. Apart from key recovery, Liminal also provides inheritance solutions for crypto owners, providing them with a way to bestow their loved ones with access to their assets in an event of their demise. In addition, the Liminal Smart Group wallets also make allowance for multiple people to operate a single wallet.

Items.Title: Django 模型：使用记录创建简单模型 - [CREATE] | Django models: Creating a simple model with records - [CREATE]
Items.Description:
阿诺·比勒陀利亚

发表于这些表和字段用于表示可以使用 Django 管理和访问的数据。 简而言之，模型定义了存储数据的结构。

让我们开始吧。

因此，假设我们想要跟踪与不同类型汽车相关的数据。

在这种情况下，我们的模型将是 Car。

下面的代码展示了我们如何创建我们的模型 Car 和它的表结构：

 # - models.py from django.db import models class Car(models.Model): type = models.CharField(max_length=30) colour = models.CharField(max_length=30)

首先，我们导航到 Django 中的 models.py 文件并导入模型。

然后我们创建我们的模型，也称为类。 由于我们的模型是 Car，因此将其命名为合适的名称。

然后我们创建我们的字段，我们有两个，即类型和颜色。 接下来，重要的是决定我们字段的数据将以何种形式存储。 由于我们只是简单地存储字符串，我们可以使用 CharField 并将 max_length 设置为 30 个字符。

一旦我们完成了我们的模型，重要的是我们迁移并推送我们的更改。

为此，我们执行以下命令：

 python manage.py makemigrations

完毕！ 下一步涉及通过调用 create() 函数将记录添加到我们的表中，这有助于我们从上面填充表。

 Car.objects.create(type='ferrari', colour='red') Car.objects.create(type='ford', colour='blue') Car.objects.create(type='nissan', colour='black') Car.objects.create(type='toyota', colour='white')

Arno Pretorius

Posted on Aug 2

A Django model is a built-in feature that is used to create tables and fields. These tables and fields are used to represent data that can be managed and accessed with Django. Simply put, models define the structure of the data that is stored.

Let's get started.

So, let's say that we want to keep track of data that is associated with different kinds of cars.

In this case, our model would be Car.

1) Creating a simple model in Django

The code below shows how we would create our model Car and the table structure for it:

# - models.py

from django.db import models

class Car(models.Model):
   type = models.CharField(max_length=30)
   colour = models.CharField(max_length=30)

First of all, we navigate to our models.py file in Django and import models.

We then create our model, which is also known as a class. Since our model is Car, it would be appropriate to name it that.

We then create our fields, we have two of them, namely type and colour. The id field is generated by default when we create our model.

Next, it's important to decide in what form the data for our fields will be stored. Since we are just simply storing strings we can use CharField and set a max_length of 30 characters.

Once we have completed our model it is important that we migrate and push our changes.

To do so, we perform the following commands:

python manage.py makemigrations

DONE! - Your model has been migrated and created!

2) Creating records for our table

The next step involves adding records to our tables by invoking the create() function, which helps us to populate the table from above.

Car.objects.create(type='ferrari', colour='red')

Car.objects.create(type='ford', colour='blue')

Car.objects.create(type='nissan', colour='black')

Car.objects.create(type='toyota', colour='white')

DONE! - We have created records for our Model - Car

Items.Description:
什么是响应式设计和引导程序？

在早期，我们有非常基本的网站服务基础设施，对于开发来说，HTML 就足够了，但随着时间的推移，对 Web 服务的需求不断增加，并且逐步出现了不同的技术来满足不断增长的需求，例如 CSS、JavaScript 和动态语言例如 PHP、ASP.Net、Java 等。

但真正的演变始于移动技术的进步，然后响应式网页设计的需求立即增长。 当时引入了 CSS 媒体查询来处理不同的屏幕尺寸问题，但前端开发变得非常复杂，因为每个人都有自己编写代码的天性并拥有自己的一组屏幕进行测试。 然后 Bootstrap 以革命性的响应模型（称为网格系统）落地。

开发人员只需要记住类并摆脱冗长的 CSS 属性。

最初 Bootstrap 于 2011 年 8 月推出 1.x 版本，后来他们发布了不同的版本，如 2.x、3.x 和 4.x 是开发人员主要使用的版本。 最近他们推出了新版本的 Bootstrap，即 Bootstrap 5，其中包含用于高级响应式网站设计的新类。

在 bootstrap 5 中，他们更新了徽标、新的 Offcanvas 组件、新的手风琴，他们为浮动标签、验证、简化布局等表单创造了非常棒的体验。

以前版本的 Bootstrap 不支持 RTL（从右到左）内容，这对于阿拉伯语、乌尔都语、波斯语网站来说非常重要。 现在，开发人员可以轻松地为那些基于 RTL 的语言构建网站。

在 Boostrap 5 中，他们添加了许多类并删除了版本 4 的一些旧类，因此建议在将旧版本迁移到最新版本之前研究版本 5 的文档。 完整的升级文档位于https://blog.getbootstrap.com/2021/05/05/bootstrap-5/

学习引导程序 5

因此，如果您是响应式网站设计的新手，请从那里的文档开始，我还在 Udemy Mastering Bootstrap 5 - Complete Guide Step by Step 2022中创建了非常详细的课程



原文:

What is Responsive Design & Bootstrap?

In early days we have very basic infrastructure of website services and for development HTML is enough but with the passage of time demand of web services increased and step by step different technologies came into existence to fulfill the growing need such as CSS, JavaScript and dynamic languages such as PHP, ASP.Net, Java and etc.

But the real evolution begins with the advancement of mobile technology, then the demand of responsive web design grows instantly. At that time CSS media queries are introduced to handle the different screen size issues but front end development becomes very complex as every person have it's own nature to write the code and have it's own set of screens for testing. Then Bootstrap come into ground with revolutionary responsive model which is called grid system.

Bootstrap is an opensource CSS & JS Framework for responsive website development. Bootstrap works on the model of grid system and they have defined generic breakpoints with respect to different screen sizes such as Extra Small, Small, Medium, Large, Extra Large, Extra Extra Large, Framework is packed with custom CSS classes which provides very fast and effective working model. Developers are required to just remember the classes and get rid of long CSS properties.

Bootstrap saves the time & cost in development of responsive website design as well as provide very beautiful responsive components such as Navigation Bar, Cards, Carousal, Forms and much more.

What's new in Bootstrap 5?

Initially Bootstrap has been launched in Aug, 2011 with version 1.x and later they released different versions like 2.x, 3.x and 4.x is a version which is mostly used by developers. Recently they launched the new version of Bootstrap which is Bootstrap 5 and contains new classes for advance responsive website design.

In bootstrap 5 they have updated the logo, New Offcanvas Component, New accordion and they have created very awesome experience for forms such as floating labels, validations, Simplified Layouts and etc.

Previous versions of Bootstrap didn't support RTL(Right-to-Left) Content which is very important features for Arabic,Urdu, Persian websites. Now developers can easily build websites for those languages which are RTL based.

In Boostrap 5 they added many classes and removed some old classes of version 4 so it's recommend to study the documentation of Version 5 before migrating your older version to newest version. complete upgrade documentation is available at https://blog.getbootstrap.com/2021/05/05/bootstrap-5/

Learn Bootstrap 5

Bootstrap is famous due to it's simplicity, easy working style, extensibility but this is all due to well documentation, strong community. so if you are new to responsive website design then get started with there documentation and i also created very detailed course at Udemy Mastering Bootstrap 5 - Complete Guide Step by Step 2022
